input.prompt: List all links and sections related to beaches (plages) on Lac d'Annecy. Provide the URLs and names of beach pages.

content.content:
# Lac d'Annecy Beaches

Based on the webpage content, here are the beaches listed for Lac d'Annecy:

## Beach Pages and URLs

1. **Plage de Saint-Jorioz**
   - URL: https://loisirs74.fr/plage-de-saint-jorioz
   - Description: "The only large natural sand beach on Lake Annecy, with shaded lawns, diving platform and accessible facilities"

2. **Plage d'Albigny**
   - URL: https://loisirs74.fr/plage-albigny
   - Description: "The largest public beach on Lake Annecy, with 400 meters of grassy shores facing the mountains"

3. **Plage des Marquisats**
   - URL: https://loisirs74.fr/plage-des-marquisats
   - Description: "A 200-meter grassy beach on the western shore, supervised in summer, with disability access"

All three beaches are marked as **Gratuit** (Free) and are located in the "Lacs & plages" (Lakes & Beaches) section of the guide. Saint-Jorioz offers the only natural sand option, while the Annecy locations provide grassy areas near the town center.

content.text: Let me fetch the details of two beaches to compare entry price and lifeguard supervision.

input.prompt: What is the entry price (tarif/prix d'entrée) and is there lifeguard supervision (surveillance/maître-nageur/baignade surveillée)? Also note facilities relevant to families with small children (shallow water, sand, shade, accessibility). Quote exact details.

What is the entry price (tarif/prix d'entrée) and is there lifeguard supervision (surveillance/maître-nageur/baignade surveillée)? Also note facilities relevant to families with small children (shallow water, sand, shade, accessibility). Quote exact details.

content.content:
# Plage de Saint-Jorioz: Pricing, Supervision & Family Facilities

## Entry Prices
**July-August (High Season):**
- Adults (18+): €2.60
- Children (10-17): €1.00
- Under 10: Free
- After 16:30: €1.00 (all ages)
- Season pass: €22.00

**September-June:** Free access

## Lifeguard Supervision
"Baignade surveillée par des maîtres-nageurs de 9h30 à 17h30 en juillet-août" (Lifeguards present 9:30 AM-5:30 PM during July-August only). No supervision outside peak season.

## Family-Friendly Features
- **Shallow water:** "Plage de sable avec pente douce, idéale pour les familles" (sand beach with gentle slope ideal for families)
- **Sand:** "la seule grande plage de sable naturel du lac d'Annecy" (only large natural sand beach on Lake Annecy)
- **Shade:** "vastes pelouses ombragées" (extensive shaded lawns)
- **Paddling area:** Pataugeoire (children's wading pool)
- **Play area:** "aire de jeux pour les plus jeunes" (playground for young children)
- **Accessibility:** Wheelchair ramps, amphibious chairs free in July-August, level entry

content.content:
# Plage d'Albigny: Entry Price, Lifeguard Supervision & Family Facilities

**Entry Price:**
"Gratuit" (Free). The beach offers "Accès libre · Gratuit" year-round with no admission fee.

**Lifeguard Supervision:**
"Surveillance assurée par des maîtres-nageurs en juillet-août de 11h à 19h" (Lifeguards on duty July-August, 11am-7pm). Outside this period, swimming is unsupervised.

**Family-Friendly Features:**

- **Shallow water & gentle slope:** "Le fond du lac est sablonneux avec une pente douce facilitant l'accès à l'eau pour les jeunes enfants"
- **Sand & grassy areas:** 400+ meters of "rives engazonnées et sablonneuses"
- **Shade:** "Grande pelouse ombragée par endroits avec bosquets"
- **Accessibility:** "Rampe de mise à l'eau PMR. Fauteuils flottants gratuits disponibles en juillet-août"
- **Facilities:** Three free restroom blocks, outdoor showers, children's play areas, and changing facilities

content.text:
I browsed loisirs74.fr and read the two beach pages. Here's the comparison.

## Comparison: Two beaches on Lac d'Annecy

| | **Plage de Saint-Jorioz** | **Plage d'Albigny** |
|---|---|---|
| **Entry price** | Paid in July–Aug: adults €2.60, children 10–17 €1.00, **under 10 free**, €1.00 after 16:30, season pass €22. Free Sept–June. | **Free year-round** ("Accès libre · Gratuit") |
| **Lifeguard supervision** | Yes, July–Aug only, **9:30–17:30** ("baignade surveillée par des maîtres-nageurs") | Yes, July–Aug only, **11:00–19:00** |
| **Beach type** | The only large **natural sand** beach on the lake, gentle slope | Mostly grassy shores; sandy bottom with gentle slope into the water |
| **Kid facilities** | Wading pool (pataugeoire), playground, shaded lawns | Play areas, shaded lawn in spots, showers/restrooms |
| **Accessibility** | Wheelchair ramps, free amphibious chairs (Jul–Aug), level entry | PMR launch ramp, free floating chairs (Jul–Aug) |

## Recommendation: **Plage de Saint-Jorioz**

For a family with small children, I'd choose **Saint-Jorioz**, despite the modest summer fee, because:

- **Real sand + gentle slope** — it's the only large natural sand beach on the lake, with a "pente douce, idéale pour les familles." Sand is far more comfortable for toddlers to play and crawl on than Albigny's mainly grassy shore.
- **Dedicated toddler features** — a **wading pool (pataugeoire)** and a **playground for young children**, which Albigny doesn't advertise.
- **Longer morning coverage** — lifeguards start at **9:30** vs. 11:00 at Albigny, matching the earlier rhythm of families with little ones (and both close their supervised swimming in the afternoon/evening).
- **Cost is minimal** — children under 10 are free and adults are only €2.60 (or €1.00 after 16:30), so the price barely offsets the family-oriented advantages.

Albigny is the better pick if a completely free, larger, town-adjacent grassy space is the priority — but on the two criteria that matter most for small kids (sandy shallow entry + toddler amenities), Saint-Jorioz is the stronger choice.

*Note: I only browsed and read pages — no forms submitted or bookings made. Prices and supervision hours are as listed on loisirs74.fr for the July–August 2026 season; worth reconfirming on-site as seasonal details can change.*
